titlestmt.titleproper: Empty Bottle Photograms and Related Material
Empty Bottle Photograms and Related Material
titlestmt.author: Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 9, 2025 at 00:35

did.unittitle: Empty Bottle Photograms and Related Material

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Collection that includes a complete set of the Empty Bottle Photograms series of photographs created Susannah Hays, 1998-2000, that comprise fifteen photograms of empty bottles placed on photographic paper as well as a photogram of a leaf that marks a shift in her practice to photograms of plant leaves. Material in the collection related to the series includes copy photographs of the images as color transparencies and digital surrogates as well as documentation of associated exhibitions, reviews, and publications.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Susannah Hays on the Arthur Corbitt Hoskins Memorial Fund, 2022.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Empty Bottle Photograms , 1998-2000. II. Material Related to Empty Bottle Photograms, 1998-2016.

bioghist.head: Susannah Hays (born 1959)
bioghist.p: Susannah Hays is an experimental photographer, book artist, and educator. She is known for using camera-less and historical photographic processes in her experimentation in visual media.

Hays was born in Boston, Massachusetts, and earned a Bachelor of Arts from Mills College in 1982 and a Master of Fine Arts in photography from the San Francisco Art Institute in 1991 as well as a Master of Arts in Visual Studies in 2002 and doctoral degree in Interdisciplinary Studies in 2016 from the University of California, Berkeley.

From 1990 to 2012 Hays was the sole proprietor of Works on Paper, a paper conservation laboratory specializing in the preservation of photographs and the production of Artist's books. She was also a faculty member of the San Francisco Art Institute from 2001 to 2012.
From 1991 to 1997 she was married to artist Matt Phillips. She lives in Santa Fe, New Mexico.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Susannah Hays, Empty Bottle Photograms and Related Material. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collection that includes a complete set of the "Empty Bottle Photograms" series of photographs created Susannah Hays, 1998-2000, that comprise fifteen photograms of empty bottles placed on photographic paper as well as a photogram of a leaf that marks a shift in her practice to photograms of plant leaves. Material in the collection related to the series includes copy photographs of the images as color transparencies and digital surrogates as well as documentation of associated exhibitions, reviews, and publications.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Susannah Hays, Empty Bottle Photograms and Related Material, is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 2, folder 20 (Compact disc (CD-R): Complete Bottle Series): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete set of the Empty Bottle Photograms series of photographs created Susannah Hays in 1998-2000 that comprise fifteen photograms of empty bottles placed on photographic paper as well as a photogram Seed Leaf (2000), which Hays included with the series to represent her transition to photograms of plant leaves. Most prints are signed, dated, numbered, and titled on verso in graphite except where noted in this guide. Inscriptions on some prints indicate editions of 5, 6, 10, and 25; print with smaller edition numbers reflect decisions by Hays to not print these images in larger editions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Print number 15 from an edition of 25; no graphite inscriptions on verso

did.unittitle: Material Related to Empty Bottle Photograms

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material related to the Empty Bottles Photograms that include copy photographs of the images as color transparencies and digital surrogates as well as documentation of associated exhibitions, reviews, and publications.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is organized into three subseries: Copy Photographs, Exhibitions, and Publications and Reviews.

did.unittitle: Copy Photographs
Susannah Hays, Picture postcards of Bottle No. 1 (1998) and Bottle No. 3 (1998)
San Francisco Art Institute, Picture postcard of Bottle No. 3 (1998)
Transparencies of Empty Bottle Photograms images
did.physdesc: 18 color transparencies, 1.9 x 3.5 cm, in mounts, 5.1 x 5.1 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy photographs of images in the Empty Bottle Photograms series as well as Seed Leaf (2000), a detail from Leaves: Ancestorial Portrait (2000), and a self-portrait of Hays at Berkeley, California, approximately 1998

did.unittitle: Compact disc (CD-R): Complete Bottle Series

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Digital surrogates of images in the Empty Bottle Photograms series; compact disc accompanied by a trimmed picture postcard that reproduces Bottle No. 3 (1998)

did.unittitle: Exhibitions
Everyday Constellations , Townsend Center for the Humanities, University of California, Berkeley
The Cameraless Image , Photo-Eye Gallery, Santa Fe, New Mexico
Publications and Reviews
Parabola , Volume 26, No. 2
John Corzine, Scott Fritz, Susannah Hays, "Feature Through the Looking Glass," Berkeley Science Review Journal
In/Visible Cosmos, Generative Art International Conference, Politecnico di Milano University, Italy, conference proceedings
Catherine Siphron, "Susannah Hays at Scott Nichols Gallery," Artweek
Satish Kumar, "Art & Science: The Photograms of Susannah Hay," Resurgence
Heather Snider, "Susannah Hays," Black & White Spotlight
Heather Snider, "In/Visible Cosmos"
DeWitt Cheng, "Nature Word," East Bay Express
John Rapko, "Embodied Camera," essay
Susannah Hays, "Equipoise"

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Excerpt from her dissertation: Susannah Hays, "Nature As Discourse : A Co-Evolutionary Systems Approach to Art and Environmental Design," University of California Berkeley, 2016
